item.title: Increased risk of respiratory syncytial virus infection in patients with active inflammatory bowel disease: a matched cohort study.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Immune dysregulation, immunosuppressive therapy and active inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) increase susceptibility to respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) infection. We evaluated the risk of RSV infe...

item.title: Appendicectomy in ulcerative colitis: a narrative review of epidemiology, pathophysiology, and emerging therapeutic implications.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Evidence from multiple studies shows that the appendix may play a significant role in the pathogenesis of ulcerative colitis (UC). These findings have sparked interest in the possibility of appendi...

item.title: A Data-Driven Approach Towards Understanding the Volume and Complexity of Young Adults With Chronic Medical Conditions Transitioning to Adult Healthcare: A Cross-Sectional Study.
item.description: Journal of paediatrics and child health - To describe the volume, complexity, referral patterns and pre-transition supports provided to a cohort of adolescents/young adults (AYAs) transitioning to adult healthcare services from a tertiary ...

item.title: Underevaluation of bowel urgency in clinical care: patient experiences and outcomes in ulcerative colitis: a retrospective cohort study.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Bowel urgency increases the burden of disease in patients with ulcerative colitis (UC). It is unclear how urgency impacts UC-related outcomes independent of other hallmark symptoms such as rectal b...

item.title: The association between artificial light at night and inflammatory bowel disease incidence, surgery, and health services utilization: population-based observational studies.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - The urban environment increases the risk of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Specific environmental exposures involved in IBD etiology remain unknown. We examined the association between outdoor a...

item.title: Absolute risk of malignancy by treatment in patients with Crohn's disease compared to the general population: a nationwide population-based cohort study.
item.description: The American journal of gastroenterology - Real-world data quantifying absolute cancer risk in Crohn's disease (CD) by treatment status are lacking but are essential for patient counselling. We linked nationwide Swedish register data and as...

item.title: Time trends in strategies for postoperative Crohn's disease management and long-term surgical recurrence: ENEIDA registry data.
item.description: Clinical gastroenterology and hepatology : the official clinical practice journal of the American Gastroenterological Association - The availability of biologicals has been associated with a decreased rate of first intestinal resection in patients with Crohn's disease (CD). However, its impact on postoperative recurrence (POR) ...

item.title: Age-related differences in the presentation, management, and outcomes of lower gastrointestinal bleeding: a retrospective multinational cohort study.
item.description: The Lancet regional health. Europe - Population ageing in Europe is reshaping the clinical profile and outcomes of lower gastrointestinal bleeding (LGIB), but age-related comparative data remain scarce. We aimed to compare clinical pr...
